a: Add and configure a source editor extension in your Xcode project.
Launch a special instance of Xcode to test your source editor extension.
The protocol you implement to handle command invocations in a source editor extension.
A key in the dictionary that defines a source editor command.
An object that identifies the command issued to your extension and provides the contents of the active source editor.
The protocol you implement to create Xcode source editor extensions.
A buffer you use to access and modify the text contents and text selections in a source editor.
A zero-based position in a source editor, defined by a line number and column number.
Creates a new text position for a specified line and column.
A half-open range of text in a buffer you use to select text or specify the insertion point for new text.
The current version of XcodeKit.
The version of XcodeKit included in Xcode 8.0.
Create extensions to add commands to the Xcode source editor.
Determine the version of XcodeKit available in an instance of Xcode.
The buffer of source text upon which the command can operate.
A handler to be invoked by Xcode to indicate that the invocation has been canceled by the user.
The class of the source editor command, in its attributes.
The horizontal component of a source text position.
The array of command definitions used by Xcode to associate command names with their implementation in an extension.
The identifier of the command that the user invoked.
The complete buffer’s string representation.
The Uniform Type Identifier (UTI) of the content in the buffer.
The end position of the range.
Tells the extension that it successfully launched and may begin to receive editor commands.
The identifier of the source editor command in its attributes.
The number of space characters used for indentation of the text in the buffer.
Creates a new source text position at the beginning of the source text.
Creates a source text position at the specified line and column.
Creates the key for a source-editor command by using the string value you specify.
Creates a new source text range defined by its starting and ending positions.
The vertical component of a source text position.
The lines of text in the buffer, including line endings.
The name of the source editor command in its attributes.
Performs the action associated with the command using the information in an invocation.
The text selections in the buffer.
The start position of the range.
An SF Symbol from the system associated with the command.
The number of space characters represented by a tab character in the buffer.
A Boolean value that indicates whether tabs are used for indentation.
